{{Short description="Second-lightest quark flavour; constituent of protons and neutrons}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Down quark
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Quark]], [[Elementary particle]], [[Proton]], [[Neutron]], [[Standard Model]]
| composition = [[Elementary particle]]
| statistics = [[Fermion]]
| symbol = d
| generation = First
| charge = −1/3 e
| mass = 4.7 ± 0.5 MeV/c²
| spin = 1/2
}}

The '''down quark''' (symbol: <math>d</math>) is a first-generation [[quark]] with [[electric charge]] −1/3 e. It is the second-lightest quark after the [[up quark]] and a fundamental constituent of ordinary [[matter]].

== Role in matter ==
Along with the up quark, the down quark forms the stable matter of the [[universe]]:

* [[Proton]] (<math>uud</math>): one down quark
* [[Neutron]] (<math>udd</math>): two down quarks

The stability of the proton (and thus all atoms) depends on the fact that the proton is the lightest [[baryon]] — free protons cannot decay into lighter particles because [[baryon number]] is conserved.

== Properties ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Property !! Value
|-
| Mass || 4.7 ± 0.5 MeV/c²
|-
| Charge || −1/3 e
|-
| [[Spin (physics)|Spin]] || 1/2
|-
| [[Isospin]] (<math>I_3</math>) || −1/2
|-
| [[Colour charge]] || Red, green, or blue
|}

== Weak decay ==
The down quark is slightly heavier than the up quark, enabling [[beta decay]]:

:<math>d \rightarrow u + e^- + \bar{\nu}_e</math> (β⁻ decay)

This is the fundamental process underlying the decay of the free [[neutron]]:
:<math>udd \rightarrow uud + e^- + \bar{\nu}_e</math>

In a bound neutron (within a nucleus), this process may be energetically forbidden, stabilising nuclei.

== CKM matrix ==
The down quark mixes with [[strange quark|strange]] and [[bottom quark]]s through the [[Cabibbo–Kobayashi–Maskawa matrix]] (CKM matrix). The <math>d \leftrightarrow u</math> transition has probability <math>|V_{ud}|^2 \approx 0.949</math>.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Beta decay]]
* [[Elementary particle]]
* [[Neutron]]
* [[Proton]]
* [[Quark]]
* [[Up quark]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Quarks]]
[[Category:Elementary particles]]
[[Category:Particle physics]]
